planContent: # 🏗️ Architecture Blueprint ## App Overview A high-fidelity, single-page interactive landing experience for the Porsche 911 GT3 RS, replicating the "Stitch AI" aesthetic. The application serves as a visual showcase of automotive engineering, utilizing a dark-themed, motion-focused layout to highlight performance specs and imagery. ## Core Features - **Hero Display**: Full-viewport atmospheric banner with a gradient-overlay title. - **Performance HUD**: Data-grid component displaying core technical specifications (speed, power, acceleration). - **Kinetic Gallery**: Asymmetric grid layout for featured vehicle components (cockpit, wheels, aero). - **Performance Narrative**: Text-image pairing section detailing the vehicle’s engineering philosophy. - **Final CTA**: High-contrast, dual-action engagement module. - **Persistent Navigation**: Glass-morphism sticky header with responsive menu toggles. ## UI & Layout Strategy - **Layout type**: Full-bleed responsive landing page. - **Header**: Sticky `glass-nav` (blur-2xl) containing the brand mark, centered logo, and utility nav icons. - **Main content area**: Stacked vertical sections following the provided Stitch design structure. - **Controls**: Primary CTA buttons use `primary-gradient`, secondary buttons use `surface-container-high`. - **Footer**: Brand-centric legal/utility footer with minimalist typography. - **Design System**: Strict Tailwind utility classes provided in the reference code (`primary-gradient`, `kerning-tight`, etc.). - **Color scheme**: Custom palette (e.g., `#131313` background, `#d5001c` primary-container, `#e2e2e2` on-surface). - **Responsive strategy**: Mobile (375px) uses a single-column layout with vertical stacking; Desktop (1440px) uses complex CSS grids for the kinetic gallery. - **Animations & UX**: Tailwind transitions on hover (`duration-700`, `scale-105`), subtle scroll-based content revealing. - **Touch Targets**: Min 44px height for all interactive buttons. - **Accessibility**: ARIA labels on all navigation items and images; `focus:ring-2` for all interactive elements. ## Data Model & State | Variable | Type | Default | Description | |----------|------|---------|-------------| | activeView | String | 'home' | Tracks current visible section | | menuOpen | Boolean | false | Mobile navigation toggle state | | imagesLoaded | Boolean | false | Triggers entrance animations | - **Persistence Strategy**: Stateless UI; state is derived from URL hash or component flags. ## Technical Stack & Libraries - **Required CDNs**: - `https://cdn.tailwindcss.com?plugins=forms,container-queries` - `https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Space+Grotesk:wght@300;400;500;600;700&family=Inter:wght@300;400;500;600;700&display=swap` - `https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Material+Symbols+Outlined:wght,FILL@100..700,0..1&display=swap` - **Allowed**: Tailwind CSS, Lucide Icons (as fallback for Material Symbols). - **APIs**: None (100% offline). ## Implementation Steps 1. Initialize HTML5 boilerplate with the specific Google Fonts and Tailwind configuration. 2. Inject `` into the head. 3. Build the `header` with sticky positioning and the defined glass-morphism style. 4. Implement the Hero Section: Add the background image with `referrerpolicy="no-referrer"` and overlay the primary headline. 5. Build the Performance HUD section using a flex-based grid for the stats. 6. Construct the Kinetic Gallery with the specified asymmetric grid layout. 7. Implement the "Engineered for Excellence" text/image pairing section. 8. Build the Final CTA section with centered, high-contrast buttons. 9. Create the Footer with the required legal links and copyright info. 10. Apply all custom CSS styles (e.g., `.primary-gradient`, `.glass-nav`) into a `
